commit.message:
[Sprint 1] Merge: QA E2E tests (237 cases)

- header.spec.cy.js: 67 tests (desktop/mobile nav, language, sticky, CTA)
- footer.spec.cy.js: 82 tests (responsive, widgets, social, accessibility)
- floating-contact.spec.cy.js: 88 tests (visibility, positioning, interactions)
- Total: 237 end-to-end test cases
- Coverage: Responsive design (375px, 768px, 1024px, 1440px)
- Accessibility: Keyboard navigation, ARIA labels, semantic HTML
- Performance: Load time, console errors, PHP warnings

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

[Sprint 1] Merge: Floating contact buttons

- Convert FloatingContact.tsx to parts/floating-contact.php
- 3 buttons (Facebook, Zalo, Phone) with icons
- Mobile/tablet only (hidden on 1024px+)
- Hover effects and animations (scale-110, ping)
- Tooltips and ARIA labels
- Uses wataco_get_floating_contacts() helper
- Polylang integrated labels

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

[Sprint 1] Merge: Footer template with widgets

- Convert Footer.tsx to footer.php
- 4-column layout on desktop, responsive on mobile
- Widget support (footer-1 through footer-4 sidebars)
- Social links and contact information
- Dynamic copyright year
- WCAG 2.1 AA compliant

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

strengthen planning.instructions.md with explicit creation and tracking rules

Add seven explicit, actionable rules for how plan documents must be
created and tracked in this repo:

- Location rule: all plans must live under docs/
- Naming rule: kebab-case filenames ending in -plan.md
- README registration rule: every plan must be added to README.md
  Plans section immediately (required, not optional)
- Required sections rule: Goal/Purpose, Pre-Implementation Change
  Review, at least one Phase, Acceptance Criteria, Last updated marker
- Status marker rule: use ✅ Done / ⏳ Pending in headings; keep them
  current
- Completion rule: move README entry to Completed when all phases done
- Pre-implementation gate rule: Pre-Implementation Change Review is a
  blocking gate — agents must validate repo state before coding

Also adds a full plan template for copy-paste use when creating new
plans. Existing valid content (keep-plans-current guidance) retained
and updated to stay consistent.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix: prevent "(default)" label from being used as model ID

When the server sends model:"(default)" in session_ready, it was stored
as selectedModel and later passed to the SDK on reconnect, causing
"Model (default) is not available" errors.

commit.message:
fix: summary agent now includes all agent types per symbol

get_recent_activities_by_symbol() was fetching TOP N activities per
symbol without grouping by agent_type. When a symbol had both put
and call watching, the most recent N activities could all be from
one type, leaving the other completely absent from summaries.

Changed to query each agent type (covered_call, cash_secured_put,
open_call_monitor, open_put_monitor) separately per symbol, then
merge and sort by timestamp. This ensures all active agent types
are represented in summary data.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add homepage deployment marker

commit.message:
Fix similar TooManySponsoring issue in revoke sponsorship

RevokeSponsorship's transfer and establish paths used ? on
update_num_sponsoring/apply_entry_sponsorship_with_sponsor, which
propagated TxError::Internal (becoming TxInternalError) instead of
returning OpTooManySponsoring when num_sponsoring was at u32::MAX.

Add pre-checks matching stellar-core's canTransferSponsorshipHelper and
canEstablishSponsorshipHelper pattern: verify num_sponsoring capacity
before mutating state. Affects all four sponsorship paths (ledger entry
transfer, ledger entry establish, signer transfer, signer establish).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Try fixing the loadtests with connection reuse for UWSGI services (#81)

# Changes


## Affected services
- bridge
- city_pass
- construction_work
- contact
- core
- image
- modules
- notification
- survey
- waste

## Definition of done
- [ ] API is backwards compatible (`make openapi-diff`)
- [ ] Swagger UI up-to-date & tested (`make dev`)
- [ ] Dependencies updated (`make requirements`)
- [ ] Infrastructure config updated (aapp_azure_infra)
- [ ] Loadtests for relevant endpoints (aapp_testing_loadtests)

After PR created (and deployed on dev):
- [ ] Manual check on dev (o) e.g. use feature via Swagger or admin
panel
- [ ] Sanity check on test app (check that dev environment is selected)

## Other notes

GitHub Copilot was used in writing the code

commit.message:
Notify fetching_envelopes when rejecting insane quorum set

When handle_quorum_set() rejects an insane quorum set, forward it to
herder.recv_quorum_set() so fetching_envelopes cleans up its fetcher
tracking for that hash. Without this, envelopes blocked on the insane
quorum set remain stranded in the fetching state until slot cleanup.

Matches stellar-core's discardSCPEnvelopesWithQSet behavior in
PendingEnvelopes::recvSCPQuorumSet.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(#88): address code review — word boundaries, code fences, perf

- Pre-compile all regexes at file level (DOLLAR_WRAPPED_REPLACEMENTS,
  BARE_LATEX_REPLACEMENTS) — zero per-token compilation cost
  \le corrupting \left, \in corrupting \input, etc. (CRITICAL)
- Skip lines inside fenced code blocks (HIGH)
- Sort replacements longest-first at compile time (MEDIUM)
- Use Regex.escapeReplacement() for Unicode chars in replace (safety)

commit.message:
Phase 6 Step 6: make accessor helpers Value::Property-aware

Update get_getter, get_setter, has_getter, has_setter, lookup_getter, and
lookup_setter to detect inline accessors in Value::Property before falling
back to legacy __get_<key>/__set_<key> hidden-key lookups.

This is safe because the GetProperty handler now handles Value::Property
with an early return (Step 3), so the legacy accessor path never executes
for Value::Property values.  Other callers either short-circuit on
contains_key(key) or are in contexts (private fields) that don't use
Value::Property.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Distinguish TooManySponsoring from LowReserve in trust flag revoke

When AllowTrust/SetTrustLineFlags revokes authorization and redeems
pool-share trustlines into claimable balances, sponsorship sandwich
creation could fail because num_sponsoring is at u32::MAX. Previously
this was unconditionally mapped to LowReserve; now it correctly
propagates as OpTooManySponsoring, matching stellar-core.

Also replaces panic on non-sandwich sponsorship exhaustion with a
proper TooManySponsoring error return.

Fixes #1198

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: henyey
repository.description: A pure Rust implementation of Stellar Core

commit.message:
Fix off-by-one in offer crossing limit

Move maxOffersToCross check to BEFORE cross_offer_v10(), matching
stellar-core which checks offerTrail.size() >= maxOffersToCross before
crossing. Previously the check was after crossing, causing the exact
1000th crossing to be rejected with opEXCEEDED_WORK_LIMIT when
stellar-core allows it.

Fixes #1212

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

rounded rectangle. Moving them to the outer relative div lets them

fix(#88): LaTeX-to-Unicode conversion in markdown renderer

LLMs commonly emit LaTeX notation in text responses ($\rightarrow$,
\times, \alpha, etc.). Our MarkdownRenderer had no LaTeX handling,
so these appeared as raw text like "$\rightarrow$".

Added convertLatexToUnicode() preprocessing step with 70+ mappings:
- Arrows: \rightarrow→, \Rightarrow⇒, \leftrightarrow↔, etc.
- Math operators: \times×, \div÷, \pm±, \cdot·
- Comparisons: \leq≤, \geq≥, \approx≈, \neq≠
- Set/logic: \in∈, \forall∀, \exists∃, \cup∪
- Greek letters: \alpha α through \Omega Ω (16 common letters)
- Miscellaneous: \infty∞, \sqrt√, \sum∑, \checkmark✓

Handles both inline math ($\cmd$) and bare commands (\cmd).
Complex LaTeX (fractions, superscripts) is left as-is since it
can't be meaningfully rendered as plain Unicode text.

Closes #88

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/elasticsoftwarefoundation/akces-framework/sessions/01087ad6-e0f9-4913-a328-ff75494a706e

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: jwijgerd <914840+jwijgerd@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(#15): title generation robustness — mutex retry, delay, logging

Three fixes for dynamic title generation not triggering reliably:

1. Changed messageCount == 4 to >= 4 so the trigger isn't missed
   if the count jumps past 4 (rapid messages, race condition)

2. Added 500ms delay before generateTitle() to let the generate()
   flow fully release LlmDispatcher and unlock the generationMutex.
   Both share a single-threaded executor — the delay ensures the
   awaitClose cleanup completes before generateOnce claims the thread.

3. Replaced generationMutex.withLock in generateOnce() with a
   tryLock + retry loop (20 attempts × 250ms = 5s max wait). This
   avoids a potential deadlock on the single-threaded dispatcher
   where withLock suspends but the unlock callback also needs the
   same thread. If the mutex is still held after 5s, logs a warning
   and returns empty string instead of hanging.

Also added comprehensive debug logging throughout the title
generation pipeline — raw model output, prompt details, and
failure reasons are now visible via: adb logcat -s KernelAI

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Phase 6 Step 5: remove redundant hidden-key markers from Value::Property sites

Now that mark helpers read inline PropAttrs from Value::Property, the
legacy hidden-key markers alongside Value::Property are redundant:

- vm.rs: remove mark_nonenumerable for fn_proto 'arguments' and 'caller'
  (inline attrs: PropAttrs::CONFIGURABLE already encodes non-enumerable)
- vm.rs: remove mark_nonenumerable for AbstractModuleSource toStringTag
- runner.rs: remove write_attrs_to_legacy_map for strict arguments 'callee'
  (inline attrs: PropAttrs::empty() already encodes non-enum, non-config)

This eliminates 5 hidden-key entries per fn_proto / arguments object.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
